text.title: Use Case Modeling Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every use-case set enforces.

rule.statement: Each use case names primary_actor as role + scope ("Authenticated Customer", "Compliance Officer") and lists secondary_actors. Anonymous "User" is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous actors collapse authority distinctions and hide multi-actor flows.

rule.statement: Each use case includes: main_success_scenario (≥3 numbered steps), ≥1 alternative_flow, ≥1 exception_flow. Single-flow use cases are rejected.
rule.rationale: Real use cases bifurcate. Single-flow descriptions hide failure paths that QA must cover.

rule.statement: preconditions and postconditions are expressed as machine-checkable predicates (boolean expressions or state assertions), not prose ("User is logged in" → "session.authenticated == true").
rule.rationale: Prose conditions drift from implementation; predicates can be enforced at runtime.

rule.statement: One use case carries exactly one user goal. "Book appointment AND pay AND email confirmation" is three use cases — split before continuing.
rule.rationale: Multi-goal use cases tangle test scope and AC derivation.

rule.statement: Each use case carries trace_id linking to the upstream story-map node or product-backlog item. Untraced use cases are rejected.
rule.rationale: Untraced use cases drift from product intent and cannot be reconciled at release planning.

example.title: Use-case fragment satisfying r1-r3
example.code: id: uc-book-appointment trace_id: PROD-432 primary_actor: Authenticated Patient secondary_actors: [Clinic Scheduling Service, SMS Gateway] preconditions: - "session.authenticated == true" - "patient.has_active_insurance == true" postconditions: - "appointment.status == 'booked'" - "sms_sent == true" main_success_scenario: 1. Patient selects clinician + slot 2. System reserves slot 3. System dispatches SMS confirmation 4. System returns booking id

example.title: Anonymous + prose conditions
example.code: title: User books appointment precondition: User is logged in flow: They pick a time and we book it
